speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Well, thou shalt see, thy eyes shall be thy judge,

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: The difference of old Shylock and Bassanio.—

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: What, Jessica!—Thou shalt not gormandize

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: As thou hast done with me—what, Jessica!—

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: And sleep, and snore, and rend apparel out.—

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Why, Jessica, I say!

speaker: LANCELET
text: Why, Jessica!

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Who bids thee call? I do not bid thee call.

speaker: LANCELET
text: Your Worship was wont to tell me I could

speaker: LANCELET
text: do nothing without bidding.

speaker: JESSICA
text: Call you? What is your will?

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: I am bid forth to supper, Jessica.

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: There are my keys.—But wherefore should I go?

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: I am not bid for love. They flatter me.

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: But yet I’ll go in hate, to feed upon

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: The prodigal Christian.—Jessica, my girl,

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Look to my house.—I am right loath to go.

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: There is some ill a-brewing towards my rest,

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: For I did dream of money bags tonight.

speaker: LANCELET
text: I beseech you, sir, go. My young master

speaker: LANCELET
text: doth expect your reproach.

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: So do I his.

speaker: LANCELET
text: And they have conspired together—I will

speaker: LANCELET
text: not say you shall see a masque, but if you do, then it

speaker: LANCELET
text: was not for nothing that my nose fell a-bleeding on

speaker: LANCELET
text: Black Monday last, at six o’clock i’ th’ morning,

speaker: LANCELET
text: falling out that year on Ash Wednesday was four

speaker: LANCELET
text: year in th’ afternoon.

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: What, are there masques? Hear you me, Jessica,

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Lock up my doors, and when you hear the drum

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: And the vile squealing of the wry-necked fife,

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Clamber not you up to the casements then,

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Nor thrust your head into the public street

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: To gaze on Christian fools with varnished faces,

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: But stop my house’s ears (I mean my casements).

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Let not the sound of shallow fopp’ry enter

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: My sober house. By Jacob’s staff I swear

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: I have no mind of feasting forth tonight.

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: But I will go.—Go you before me, sirrah.

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Say I will come.

speaker: LANCELET
text:
I will go before, sir.
                 
                Aside
                 
                to
                 
                Jessica
                .
               Mistress,

speaker: LANCELET
text: look out at window for all this.

speaker: LANCELET
text: There will come a Christian by

speaker: LANCELET
text: Will be worth a Jewess’ eye.

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: What says that fool of Hagar’s offspring, ha?

speaker: JESSICA
text: His words were “Farewell, mistress,” nothing else.

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: The patch is kind enough, but a huge feeder,

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Snail-slow in profit, and he sleeps by day

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: More than the wildcat. Drones hive not with me,

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Therefore I part with him, and part with him

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: To one that I would have him help to waste

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: His borrowed purse. Well, Jessica, go in.

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Perhaps I will return immediately.

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Do as I bid you. Shut doors after you.

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: Fast bind, fast find—

speaker: SHYLOCK
text: A proverb never stale in thrifty mind.

speaker: JESSICA
text: Farewell, and if my fortune be not crossed,

speaker: JESSICA
text: I have a father, you a daughter, lost.
